metadata.title: WCH CH32V203C8T6
metadata.description: Draft HAIR extraction for the WCH CH32V203C8T6 using official WCH datasheet/reference manuals and the official openwch SDK.

evidence.name: Datasheet scope metadata
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official WCH CH32V203DS0 datasheet v3.0 explicitly covers CH32V203C8T6.

evidence.name: Model comparison table
evidence.normalizedClaim: CH32V203C8x6 devices provide 64KB flash, 20KB SRAM, 37 GPIOs, 4 USART/UART, 2 SPI, 2 I2C, 1 CAN, 2 ADC/TKey units, and 2 OPA/CMP units.

evidence.name: Architecture overview
evidence.normalizedClaim: CH32V203 is based on the QingKe V4B 32-bit RISC-V core and supports up to 144MHz system frequency.

evidence.name: Clock and supply facts
evidence.normalizedClaim: The device uses 2.4V to 3.6V system supply, built-in 8MHz HSI RC, built-in 40KHz LSI RC, external 3MHz to 25MHz HSE, external 32.768KHz LSE, and PLL-derived SYSCLK up to 144MHz.

evidence.name: Memory map figure
evidence.normalizedClaim: The family memory map places SRAM at 0x20000000, APB/AHB peripheral space at 0x40000000, and USBFS at 0x50000000.

evidence.name: Package and ordering table
evidence.normalizedClaim: CH32V203C8T6 is offered in the LQFP48 7x7mm package with 0.5mm pin pitch.

evidence.name: C8T6 OSC pin note
evidence.normalizedClaim: For CH32V203C8T6, pins 5 and 6 default to OSC_IN and OSC_OUT after reset and can be remapped to PD0 and PD1 by software.

evidence.name: C8T6 linker memory layout
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official CH32V203C8T6 example linker script places the 64KB flash application image at the 0x00000000 boot alias and 20KB RAM at 0x20000000 for the D6 C8 group.

evidence.name: Header D6 grouping
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official header maps CH32V20x_D6 to CH32V203C6 and CH32V203C8 class parts, including CH32V203C8T6.

evidence.name: Peripheral base macros
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official header defines the CH32V20x D6 peripheral base addresses used for the structural peripheral map.

evidence.name: IRQ enumeration
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official header enumerates the CH32V20x_D6 interrupt identifiers used by the C8T6 variant.

evidence.name: Startup vector table
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official D6 startup file defines the C8T6-class external interrupt vector ordering from WWDG through DMA1_Channel8.

evidence.name: Reference manual overview
evidence.normalizedClaim: The CH32FV2x_V3xRM reference manual is the official register and peripheral manual for CH32V203C8T6-class devices.

evidence.name: QingKe V4 processor scope
evidence.normalizedClaim: The QingKe V4 processor manual covers V4A/V4B/V4C/V4F cores and provides ISA-level details for the CH32V203C8T6 QingKe V4B core.

evidence.name: SDK compiler architecture flags
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official CH32V203C8T6 example build compiles for -march=rv32imacxw and -mabi=ilp32, with no floating-point ISA extension enabled.

evidence.name: LQFP48 pin definition table
evidence.normalizedClaim: Table 3-1-1 defines the CH32V203 LQFP48 package pad assignments, default functions, and alternate/remap functions used for the CH32V203C8T6 pin model.

provenance.notes: The datasheet maps the underlying code flash at 0x08000000 and describes the 0x00000000 region as a boot-time alias to flash or system memory depending on BOOT pins; the official linker script uses the flash alias at 0x00000000.

interruptControllers.name: PFIC
interruptControllers.description: Fast programmable interrupt controller integrated with the QingKe V4B core.

pins.name: VDD
pins.description: Digital supply pin group used by pads labeled VDD, VDD_2, VDD_IO_1, and VDD_IO_3.

pins.name: VSS
pins.description: Digital ground pin group used by pads labeled VSS, VSS_1, VSS_2, and VSS_3.

pins.name: PC13
pins.description: GPIO pin with TAMPER-RTC alternate function; output use is limited to 2MHz and light loads per datasheet note 3.

pins.name: PC14
pins.description: GPIO/LSE input pin; when VBAT powers the backup domain, this pad is reserved for LSE use.

pins.name: PC15
pins.description: GPIO/LSE output pin; when VBAT powers the backup domain, this pad is reserved for LSE use.

pins.name: PD0
pins.description: Defaults to OSC_IN after reset on CH32V203C8T6 and can be remapped to GPIO PD0 by software.

pins.name: PD1
pins.description: Defaults to OSC_OUT after reset on CH32V203C8T6 and can be remapped to GPIO PD1 by software.

pins.name: PB2
pins.description: PB2 is package-shared with BOOT1 and should be pulled down in low-power configurations when unused.

packages.name: LQFP48
packages.description: 7x7 mm LQFP48 package with 0.5 mm pitch used by CH32V203C8T6.
